title: Public Feedback Infrastructure Pack
context: GrantLayer is in public Developer Preview on GitHub. GL-187 through GL-191 delivered public docs cleanup, a first-output verify helper, a grant lifecycle evidence bundle, a demo endpoint safety guard, and a developer experience polish pack. GL-192 formalises the public feedback infrastructure: intake categories, severity model, label taxonomy plan, issue template guidance, security-sensitive routing, and round-2 reviewer guidance.

public_feedback_categories.name: quickstart-feedback
public_feedback_categories.description: Friction, confusion, or errors when following README or agent_quickstart.md
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: public issue okay with synthetic data

public_feedback_categories.name: first-output-feedback
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: public issue okay with synthetic data

public_feedback_categories.name: grant-lifecycle-example-feedback
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: public issue okay with synthetic data

public_feedback_categories.name: developer-experience-feedback
public_feedback_categories.description: General DX pain: setup, error messages, tooling, agent workflow
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: public issue okay with synthetic data

public_feedback_categories.name: bug-report
public_feedback_categories.description: Reproducible software bug using synthetic or demo data
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: public issue okay with synthetic data only

public_feedback_categories.name: feature-request
public_feedback_categories.description: Scoped improvement suggestion for Developer Preview stage

public_feedback_categories.name: product-question
public_feedback_categories.description: Questions about roadmap, architecture, tenant model, production posture
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: public issue okay; answer must preserve Developer Preview caveats

public_feedback_categories.name: security-sensitive-report
public_feedback_categories.description: Vulnerability, auth bypass, secret exposure, audit tampering risk, data leakage
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: GitHub Security Advisories only — do not file publicly

public_feedback_categories.name: non-scope-later
public_feedback_categories.description: Production SaaS deployment, real multi-tenant isolation, customer data handling
public_feedback_categories.template: none — deferred out of Developer Preview scope
public_feedback_categories.safe_submission: deferred — not applicable at this stage

definitions.critical: Production-blocking security issue (auth bypass, secret leak, data exposure). Route to GitHub Security Advisories immediately.
definitions.high: Significant correctness failure, misleading public safety statement, or security-adjacent concern not yet critical.
definitions.medium: Reproducible bug that degrades a documented workflow; confusing or incorrect documentation that could mislead developers.
definitions.low: Minor friction, cosmetic issues, non-blocking doc gaps.
definitions.info: General feedback, questions, observations with no actionable bug.

routing.critical: GitHub Security Advisories — private coordinated disclosure only
routing.info: public issue — product question, DX feedback, or docs feedback

label_taxonomy_plan.note: All labels are recommended only. They must be created manually in GitHub repository settings if maintainers choose to adopt them. No labels are created or modified via the GitHub API by GL-192 or any automated process.

templates_in_place.note: This template explicitly redirects to GitHub Security Advisories and instructs reporters not to open public issues for sensitive findings.
Disables blank issues and adds contact links to security policy and agent docs.

required_safety_content: Do not include secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys)
Do not include real customer data
Do not include private grants or institutional data
Do not include exploit details in public issues
Use synthetic or demo data only
For security-sensitive reports, use GitHub Security Advisories
issue_template_guidance.developer_preview_caveat_required: Developer Preview — GrantLayer is not production SaaS. Tenant isolation is not implemented.

what_routes_to_advisory: secret, token, or credential exposure in tracked files or outputs
audit log tampering or suppression vectors
exploit reproduction details, payloads, or proof-of-concept code
sensitive system information (internal paths, infrastructure details)
what_public_issues_may_include: high-level description of the type of concern without exploit details
request for a private reporting path if Security Advisories are unavailable
never_in_any_report: real secrets, API tokens, or credentials
real customer data, real grant applications, institutional records
private personal data (names, addresses, identifiers of real individuals)
internal infrastructure paths, hostnames, or private remotes

safe_feedback_instructions: Use synthetic or demo data only — do not use real customer data, real grant applications, or real institutional records.
Do not include secrets, tokens, API keys, passwords, or private keys in any public issue body, comment, or attachment.
Do not include exploit details, payloads, or sensitive reproduction steps in public issues. File those privately via GitHub Security Advisories.
For product questions that assume production SaaS or tenant isolation: note the current caveat in your question.
For security-sensitive reports: use GitHub Security Advisories (https://github.com/Discodone/grantlayer/security/advisories), not public issues.
Choose the correct issue template for your category.
Complete all required checklist items in the issue template safety checklist.

what_reviewers_can_try.name: First-output verify helper
what_reviewers_can_try.expected: deterministic JSON output, no backend or secrets required

what_reviewers_can_try.name: Grant lifecycle evidence bundle
what_reviewers_can_try.expected: full grant lifecycle simulation with audit chain, no backend required

what_reviewers_can_try.name: Quickstart
what_reviewers_can_try.command: see README — What to try first section
what_reviewers_can_try.expected: clear entry path to both no-install examples

what_reviewers_can_try.name: Agent task workflow
what_reviewers_can_try.expected: clear agent operating rules and task contract format

how_to_report: Use the appropriate issue template (bug, docs, DX feedback, feature request).
Use synthetic or demo data only.
For security-sensitive concerns, use GitHub Security Advisories.
what_reviewers_should_not_do: Do not provide real customer data, real grant applications, or real institutional records.
Do not include real secrets, tokens, API keys, or passwords.
Do not include exploit details in public issue bodies.
Do not assume GrantLayer is production SaaS.
Do not assume tenant isolation is implemented.

findings.title: product-question category has no dedicated issue template
findings.detail: The product-question feedback category does not have a dedicated .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE file. Reporters should open a plain issue with the label type:question. This is acceptable at Developer Preview stage.
findings.action: documented as intended — no template required

findings.title: Label taxonomy is recommended only — not yet created in GitHub
findings.detail: The full label taxonomy defined in GL-192 has not been applied to the GitHub repository. Labels must be created manually if maintainers choose to adopt them. No automated API changes are performed.
findings.action: documented as intended — manual label creation is a future optional step

next_recommended_step: GL-192 Combined Merge-and-Publish for Public Feedback Infrastructure Pack — merge gl-192-public-feedback-infrastructure-pack to internal main, then run the snapshot publish process to push the feedback infrastructure documentation to the public GitHub repository.
non_goals: Creating GitHub issues or pull requests via API
Modifying backend/src, OpenAPI, migrations, DB/schema, dependency manifests, SDK, examples runtime, frontend, website, design
Changing GitHub repository settings or visibility
Changing GitHub Actions or workflow files
Requesting real customer data, private grants, secrets, or exploit details
Adding paperclip or external SaaS status updates
